commands.review_summary:
The PR threads ClickClack account `toolsAllow` through inbound/channel reply dispatch into agent execution and adds regression coverage for that path.

PR surface: Source +28, Tests +77. Total +105 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: configure a ClickClack account with `toolsAllow`, then follow the agent reply path through `runtime.channel.inbound.dispatchReply` and the exported provider dispatcher wrapper; the wrapper does not forward the new field into lower-level dispatch.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The code blocker is narrow, but the PR also needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof for the corrected dispatch path before merge.

target.title: Propagate ClickClack tool policy through reply dispatch
target.body:
## Summary
- Propagates ClickClack account tool policy through inbound agent reply dispatch
- Threads runtime tool restrictions through shared reply dispatch into agent execution

## Changes
- Adds a runtime `toolsAllow` handoff on shared inbound dispatch params
- Carries the policy through reply options into embedded and CLI agent execution
- Adds regression coverage for ClickClack dispatch, shared dispatch, and embedded agent execution

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: ClickClack account-level tool allowlists are carried into the agent reply runtime so the account policy remains the effective runtime tool set.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw worktree `~/Worktrees/openclaw/openclaw/fix/fix-708` at commit `8a5cc59`, Node `v22.22.2`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a production embedded-agent tool-policy boundary probe with `node --import tsx` from the PR worktree after the patch.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied console output from the local OpenClaw run:

```text
[proof-708] command=node --import tsx <production embedded-agent tool-policy boundary>
[proof-708] runtimeToolsAllow=["message"]
[proof-708] constructionPlan={"constructTools":true,"includeCoreTools":true,"runtimeToolAllowlist":["message"],"codingToolConstructionPlan":{"includeBaseCodingTools":false,"includeShellTools":false,"includeChannelTools":false,"includeOpenClawTools":true,"includePluginTools":false}}
[proof-708] availableTools=["message","exec","read","web_search"]
[proof-708] allowedToolsAfterRuntimePolicy=["message"]
[proof-708] bundleMcpRuntimeCreated=false
[proof-708] result=pass
```

- Observed result after fix: The runtime allowlist was `["message"]`; `exec`, `read`, and `web_search` were excluded from the allowed tool set, and the broader bundle MCP runtime was not created.
- What was not tested: Live ClickClack service delivery and a real model-provider turn were not run locally; those require external service credentials.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The live proof is scoped to the production embedded-agent tool-policy boundary. Regression coverage separately exercises ClickClack dispatch propagation and shared reply dispatch propagation.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-clickclack.config.ts extensions/clickclack/src/inbound.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/dispatch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:extensions`
- `git diff --check`
- `claude -p "/review 89500"` returned `approve`

## Notes
- CLI-backed agent runs already fail closed when a runtime tool allowlist is requested, because that backend cannot enforce the runtime policy.
target.author: mmaps
